_metadata.description: Bonus-eligible standardized-extract registry. NOT a botanical identity file — identity lives in botanical_ingredients.json.
_metadata.purpose: Bonus-awarding layer (A5b_standardized_botanical = +1pt) per scripts/config/scoring_config.json:105 and score_supplements.py:1133-1186. An entry triggers the bonus ONLY when the label/entry proves a measurable marker, branded standardized extract, or quantified constituent standardization. Plain botanical aliases (e.g., 'garlic', 'turmeric root', 'green coffee bean') must NOT live here for bonus purposes — they belong in botanical_ingredients.json for identity-only mapping with no bonus. The runtime enforcement is the enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148), which require percentage detection in label text before granting the bonus; this file's contract describes the editorial discipline for which entries earn that pathway.

bonus_eligibility_contract.rule: An entry may stay in standardized_botanicals.json only if it has at least one of the four standardization bases below. If none applies, move the entry (or migrate plain aliases) to botanical_ingredients.json (basic identity, no A5b bonus).

marker_percent.description: Explicit marker standardization on the label or in entry notes — 'standardized to X%', 'minimum X%', quantified active constituent.

branded_extract.description: Verified branded standardized extract with documented marker/fingerprint. Evidence MUST be cited in entry notes/sources.

pharmacopeial_marker.description: Pharmacopeial (USP/EP/JP) extract with defined marker profile or NIH-ODS-style marker/bioactive constituent specification documented.
examples: USP Saw Palmetto Extract (85-95% fatty acids and sterols)
EP Ginkgo dry extract (22-27% flavone glycosides, 5-7% terpene lactones)

mushroom_fraction.description: Mushroom fraction standardized to beta-glucans, PSK, PSP, or other characterized fraction, with species and preparation preserved.

bonus_eligibility_contract.do_not_use_unii_alone: UNII proves IDENTITY, not standardization. A clean UNII match is necessary for an entry to ship safely, but it does NOT by itself make an entry bonus-eligible.
bonus_eligibility_contract.do_not_bulk_move: Cleanup of legacy entries must proceed in controlled 8-12 entry batches with per-entry shadow-score comparison. See scripts/audits/standardized_botanicals_eligibility_20260522/REPORT.md for prioritized cleanup batches (bladderwrack/brown_kelp, boswellia, bilberry, ginger_extract, nettle, rhodiola, grape_seed, cordyceps, turkey_tail, sea_buckthorn, turmeric).
bonus_eligibility_contract.preserve_plain_aliases_during_migration: Until the per-entry §8.5/marker-evidence audit lands, plain alias removal is too aggressive — the enricher's meets_threshold gate already filters non-standardized labels at scoring time. Premature alias removal would strip the bonus from products whose labels DO carry marker evidence but whose ingredient-row string is plain (e.g. 'Green Coffee bean extract' with a separate '50% chlorogenic acids' notes line). Plain identity in botanical_ingredients.json is a fallback home for products that should never match here, not a replacement for runtime gating.
bonus_eligibility_contract.added_by: 2026-05-22 Codex review + user directive — established after audit found that <=4% of legacy entries were clearly marker-qualified and >=66% contained §8.5 multi-UNII contamination.

per_entry_v6_schema_fields.bonus_eligible: boolean — true when one of the four standardization bases is satisfied by the entry's documented evidence
per_entry_v6_schema_fields.standardization_basis: enum — marker_percent | branded_extract | pharmacopeial_marker | extract_ratio_plus_marker | mushroom_fraction | none
per_entry_v6_schema_fields.bonus_rationale: string — 1-2 sentence explanation citing the standardization evidence
per_entry_v6_schema_fields.sources: string[] — required when bonus_eligible=true (PMID/DOI/USP monograph/NIH ODS/ABC URL)

_metadata.v6_migration_status: PHASE 1: contract metadata + green_coffee_bean tutorial entry landed 2026-05-22. green_coffee_bean is the first entry annotated with the new schema fields; plain aliases retained for runtime matching while the meets_threshold gate continues to govern bonus eligibility. Remaining priority entries per audit: bladderwrack/brown_kelp, boswellia, bilberry, ginger_extract, nettle, rhodiola, grape_seed, cordyceps, turkey_tail, sea_buckthorn, turmeric. Each ships as its own 1-entry batch with shadow-score comparison.
_metadata.scoring_rule: Botanicals with min_threshold are scored higher when meeting standardization requirements. Priority level affects matching weight. After v6 migration: only entries with bonus_eligible=true (and a valid standardization_basis) are intended to trigger the A5b bonus; the runtime meets_threshold gate is the authoritative enforcement.
_metadata.pipeline_integration: Used by cleaning script to standardize botanical ingredient names and by enrichment script to identify quality markers
_metadata.notes: | identity_bioactivity_split Phase 3: 5 source-botanical aliases relocated to source canonicals.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Antioxidant berry standardized for 1% anthocyanins. ORAC value is also used as quality marker.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|other|Haematococcus pluvialis | 2266 (Number of products:32)

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Astaxanthin sourced from Haematococcus pluvialis is the commercial natural astaxanthin pathway, with branded preparations from Cyanotech (BioAstin®), AstaReal AB (AstaReal®), and Algatech (AstaPure / Zanthin variants). Standardized to defined astaxanthin percentage (typically 1-5% in commercial extracts; 10%+ in concentrates). Clinical literature on oxidative-stress, skin, and exercise endpoints. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names BioAstin / AstaReal / AstaPure / Zanthin brand variants. Companion plain identity in botanical_ingredients.astaxanthin (no bonus). Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown. [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: AstaZine® (BGG / Algatech) is a clinically-studied Haematococcus pluvialis-derived natural astaxanthin extract standardized to 5%+ astaxanthin. Companion to standardized_botanicals.astaxanthin_haematococcus_pluvialis which owns the broader algal astaxanthin pathway. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the AstaZine® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Immune support herb standardized for 0.5% astragaloside IV or 16% polysaccharides.
Standardized botanical extract with polysaccharides, astragalosides as primary marker compounds.
Cognitive enhancement botanical standardized for bacosides. Range is 20-55% bacosides; clinical studies commonly use 300-450mg at 50% standardization.
Blood sugar support herb standardized for 1% corosolic acid (GlucoHelp standard is 1%).
Performance vegetable standardized for 0.5-1% nitrates for cardiovascular benefits.
Blood sugar support alkaloid standardized to 97% berberine HCl. Emerging alternative to metformin in metabolic health supplements.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Berberine | 1060 (Number of products:199)

standardized_botanicals.notes: Botanical ingredient standardized for beta-glucans. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown if not specified. [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Bil-Max® (Sabinsa) is a clinically-studied bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus) extract standardized to 25%+ anthocyanins (high-grade anthocyanidins). Companion to standardized_botanicals.bilberry (v6-annotated in SB-5) which already owns the bilberry pathway. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Bil-Max® brand specifically. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

Bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus, UNII 9P2U39H18W) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares a measurable anthocyanin or anthocyanoside % (typically 25-36% — 'Mirtoselect' from Indena is the original 36%-anthocyanin standardized extract used in most European clinical trials). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Bilberry' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. Preparation variants (UNIIs R911H793SU 'bilberry extract', V9S692O326 'bilberry fruit extract', KK0KHX972K 'european bilberry') are FDA-registered preparation variants of the SAME species (V. myrtillus, UNII 9P2U39H18W) — not cross-species §8.5 misplacement; retained for runtime identity matching.
sources: Indena Mirtoselect monograph (Vaccinium myrtillus standardized to 36% anthocyanins per Indena HPLC method)
Chu et al. 2011 systematic review on Mirtoselect / anthocyanin-standardized bilberry (PMID 21660670)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus, Ericaceae) fruit. UNII 9P2U39H18W. Eye-health and microcirculatory botanical standardized for anthocyanins/anthocyanosides (typical 25%; Mirtoselect 36%). SB-5 §8.5 / v6 annotation 2026-05-22: refined standard_name to include species; added v6 contract fields (bonus_eligible, standardization_basis, marker_compounds, bonus_rationale, sources); added marker-explicit alias phrasings (Mirtoselect branded + 25%/36% standardized) for direct matching of labels carrying the standardization claim. The audit's reported '4 distinct UNIIs in aliases' resolved to preparation variants of V. myrtillus (not cross-species misplacement) per the SB-4 same-species-different-UNII pattern; preparation-variant aliases retained.

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Bladderwrack (Fucus vesiculosus) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares a measurable fucoidan or iodine % (e.g., 'standardized to 1% fucoidan' or a specified mcg/iodine claim). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Bladderwrack' identity without marker evidence still matches this entry for IDENTITY purposes but does NOT trigger A5b unless meets_threshold passes. Distinct species from generic brown kelp (Laminaria, UNII 4R2124HE76) and Ascophyllum nodosum rockweed (UNII 168S4EO8YJ).
sources: NIH ODS Iodine factsheet (Fucus vesiculosus listed as natural iodine source): https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Iodine-HealthProfessional/
EFSA Scientific Opinion on the safety of fucoidan as a novel food (EFSA Panel on Nutrition 2017): https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/efsajournal/pub/4814
standardized_botanicals.notes: Bladderwrack (Fucus vesiculosus). UNII 535G2ABX9M. Standardization markers: fucoidan (sulfated polysaccharide) and iodine (natural source, varies 100-1000 mcg/g dry weight). SB-3a §7.5/§8.5 cleanup 2026-05-22: alias family migrated here from IQM brown_kelp ('Bladder Wrack', 'Bladderwrack', 'Fucus vesiculosus', 'Fucus vesiculosus extract', 'bladder wrack extract', 'bladderwrack extract'). 'Bladderwrack Whole Plant Extract' added to cover Ora pid=259887 (Phase 1 Item 3 — that label was previously misrouting to brown_kelp generic-kelp pathway). Bonus pathway: marker_percent only when the label declares the standardization; plain 'Bladderwrack' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold gate.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Blueberry | 23 (Number of products:1647)

aliases: Joint Shield 5-Loxin Advanced Bosclear Boswellia serrata extract
boswellia serrata extract standardized to 65% boswellic acids
boswellia serrata extract standardized to 70% boswellic acids
boswellia extract standardized to boswellic acids
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Boswellia serrata (UNII 4PW41QCO2M) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares a measurable boswellic-acid % (typically 65-70% total boswellic acids) OR a named branded standardized extract with documented marker profile: 5-Loxin / 5-Loxin Advanced (Sabinsa, ≥30% AKBA), Boswellin (Sabinsa, 65% boswellic acids), Bosclear, AprèsFlex (Laila Nutraceuticals, ≥20% AKBA). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Boswellia' identity without marker evidence still matches this entry but does NOT trigger A5b unless meets_threshold passes. Distinct species from Boswellia carterii (Somali frankincense, UNII R9XLF1R1WM) — that identity lives in botanical_ingredients.boswellia_carterii.
sources: Abdel-Tawab et al. 2011 systematic review on boswellic acid pharmacology (PMID 21679876)
Sabinsa 5-Loxin / Boswellin product monographs (branded standardized extracts)
Sengupta et al. 2008 osteoarthritis RCT on 5-Loxin (PMID 18667066) — AKBA-standardized B. serrata
standardized_botanicals.notes: Boswellia serrata (Burseraceae) gum resin. UNII 4PW41QCO2M. Anti-inflammatory herb. Standardization markers: total boswellic acids (typically 65-70%) plus AKBA (3-acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid) as the most active sub-marker (5-30% in branded extracts). SB-4 §8.5 cleanup 2026-05-22: refined standard_name from 'Boswellia' to species-precise 'Boswellia serrata'. Removed 'frankincense extract' alias (UNII R9XLF1R1WM = B. carterii, a different species) — that identity now lives in botanical_ingredients.boswellia_carterii. Added v6 contract fields per the bonus-eligibility governance landed in eff29b3d on main; added marker-explicit alias phrasings for direct matching of labels carrying the standardization claim. Branded extracts (5-Loxin, Joint Shield, Boswellin, Bosclear, AprèsFlex) retained — their formulation UNIIs differ from the species UNII but the species identity is B. serrata with documented marker profiles.
Cruciferous vegetable extract standardized for 0.4% sulforaphane. Used in detoxification and cancer prevention supplements.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Chromax — chromium picolinate by Nutrition21. Dosed by elemental chromium (typically 200 mcg/dose). [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Chromax® (Nutrition 21) is the original clinically-studied chromium picolinate preparation (12.43% elemental chromium by weight). Extensive clinical literature on glycemic control, insulin sensitivity, and lean-body-mass support. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Chromax® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Essential oil standardized for cinnamaldehyde, eugenol. Used for therapeutic and functional benefits.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ketosteroids. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Polyphenol-rich bergamot extract used for lipid and cardiometabolic support. Clinical literature commonly profiles neoeriocitrin, naringin, and neohesperidin in bergamot polyphenolic fractions.

Cocoa extract used for vascular and cardiometabolic support. Clinical evidence most often references daily cocoa flavanol content and epicatechin-rich profiles rather than a universal label percentage threshold.
Botanical ingredient standardized for chlorogenic acids, polyphenols. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
standardized_botanicals.threshold_source: 45% chlorogenic acids — standard coffee fruit extract

standardized_botanicals.notes: Cognigrape (Bionap branded red-grape, Vitis vinifera) anthocyanin-rich standardized extract clinically studied for cognitive support in older adults (Calapai 2017 PMID 28552883). UNII RDS2V6DVY5 (grape seed extract preparation registry — matches Cognigrape's grape-seed-pomace chemistry). SB-9 cleanup 2026-05-22: fixed copy-paste typo ('gognigrape' removed; canonical 'cognigrape' + case variants preserved); populated external_ids.unii. [PB-1 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Cognigrape® (Bionap) is an anthocyanin-rich red-grape extract (Vitis vinifera fruit/seed/skin) standardized to a defined polyphenol profile. Clinical literature on cognitive performance endpoints in healthy adults. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Cognigrape® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Cognizin — fermentation-derived citicoline by Kyowa Hakko. ≥99% purity by HPLC.
Weight management and cardiovascular herb standardized for 10% forskolin.

aliases: cordyceps sinensis extract standardized to 40% polysaccharides
cordyceps sinensis extract standardized to beta-glucans
cordyceps sinensis extract standardized to adenosine
marker_compounds: cordycepin (low in C. sinensis vs C. militaris)
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Cordyceps sinensis (Ophiocordyceps sinensis, UNII 8Q1GYP08KU) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares polysaccharides % (typical 40%+ — the historical Chinese pharmacopeial standard), beta-glucans %, adenosine %, or names the CS-4 fermented-mycelium variant (Paecilomyces hepiali Cs-4, UNII 821RF3P03C — used in most clinical trials due to wild C. sinensis being unsustainable). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Cordyceps' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. Bonus pathway is species-specific to C. sinensis — Cordyceps militaris (UNII J617U5X7NN) is a different species with a cordycepin-focused marker profile and lives in standardized_botanicals.cordyceps_militaris.
sources: Holliday & Cleaver 2008 review on Cordyceps sinensis / CS-4 pharmacology (PMID 18197785)
Chen et al. 2010 RCT on CS-4 fermented mycelium for exercise performance (PMID 20578760)
Chinese Pharmacopoeia 2020 Cordyceps sinensis monograph (40% polysaccharides minimum spec)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Cordyceps sinensis / Ophiocordyceps sinensis (Ophiocordycipitaceae). UNII 8Q1GYP08KU. Performance / adaptogenic mushroom. Standardization markers: polysaccharides (40%+ Chinese-pharmacopeial standard) plus adenosine. Note that cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine, UNII GZ8VF4M2J8) is LOW in C. sinensis — high-cordycepin products are typically C. militaris. SB-10 §8.5 species split 2026-05-22: closed the missing-UNII gap (set external_ids.unii=8Q1GYP08KU); refined standard_name to 'Cordyceps sinensis'; removed 'cordyceps militaris' alias (different species, UNII J617U5X7NN, now lives in companion standardized_botanicals.cordyceps_militaris entry); added v6 contract fields + marker-explicit alias phrasings + CS-4 fermented-mycelium alias variants.

aliases: cordyceps militaris extract standardized to 0.1% cordycepin
cordyceps militaris extract standardized to 0.3% cordycepin
cordyceps militaris extract standardized to cordycepin
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Cordyceps militaris (UNII J617U5X7NN) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares a measurable cordycepin % (typical 0.1-0.3%) — cordycepin is C. militaris's signature bioactive and the source of most ergogenic-supplement clinical interest. Beta-glucans % is the secondary marker pathway. Distinct species from Cordyceps sinensis (UNII 8Q1GYP08KU), which is polysaccharide-focused; C. militaris is commercially cultivable and contains higher cordycepin than wild C. sinensis. Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate. Plain 'Cordyceps militaris' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold.
sources: Hirsch et al. 2017 RCT on Cordyceps militaris fruiting body for high-intensity exercise (PMID 28012484)
Tuli et al. 2014 review on cordycepin pharmacology and bioavailability (PMID 23538328)
PerformanceMushrooms / Real Mushrooms C. militaris monograph (fruiting-body extract standardized to cordycepin)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Cordyceps militaris (Cordycipitaceae). UNII J617U5X7NN. Commercially cultivable performance mushroom with high cordycepin content compared to wild C. sinensis. Created 2026-05-22 via SB-10 §8.5 cleanup to host the 'cordyceps militaris' alias that previously co-existed with C. sinensis under one entry. Distinct standardization basis (cordycepin %) vs companion standardized_botanicals.cordyceps entry (polysaccharides %, beta-glucans).
Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown if not specified. [PB-1 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Cran-Max® (PharmaChem Innovations) is a clinically-studied whole-cranberry fruit extract with the entire phytochemical complement (PACs, anthocyanins, phenolics). Used in UTI-prevention RCTs (Bio-Shield™ delivery). branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Cran-Max® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: CranRx — cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) extract. Standardized to 36 mg PAC/dose by BL-DMAC method.

Curcumin marker compound (UNII IT942ZTH98, verified). Anti-inflammatory standardized to 95% curcuminoids. C3 Complex (Sabinsa), BCM-95 Bio-Curcumin (Arjuna Natural), Meriva (Indena phytosome), Longvida, Theracurmin are bonus-eligible branded standardized extracts. Plain curcuminoid powder aliases (curcumin powder, curcumin extract) safe under runtime meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148) — bonus fires only when label proves measurable curcuminoid percentage.
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Curcumin and the curcuminoid family are the bioactive principles of Curcuma longa. Clinical literature on anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, and joint-comfort endpoints uses standardized curcuminoid percentage (typically 95%+ curcuminoids for premium extracts) and quantified branded extracts (C3 Complex, BCM-95, Meriva, Longvida, Theracurmin). The marker_percent basis applies: bonus is granted only when the label proves measurable curcuminoid percentage or quantified branded-extract standardization. The enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148) enforce label-text percentage detection at runtime.

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: EPs® 7630 (Dr. Willmar Schwabe Pharmaceuticals) is a clinically-studied Pelargonium sidoides root extract standardized to a defined fingerprint of coumarins (incl. umckalin) and gallotannins. Approved as an OTC herbal medicinal product in Germany for acute bronchitis (Umckaloabo®). Extensive RCT literature on upper respiratory infections. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names EPs® 7630 / Umckaloabo® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

aliases: ginger extract standardized to 5% total gingerols and shogaols
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Ginger (Zingiber officinale, UNII C5529G5JPQ) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares a measurable gingerols / shogaols / total-pungent-compounds % (typical 5-20% gingerols, with shogaols as the secondary marker formed during drying / heating). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Ginger' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. Marker-compound UNIIs in alias list (V2ZD052TS1 'total gingerols and shogaols', SAS9Z1SVUK 'standardized ginger gingerols', 925QK2Z900 '6-gingerol') describe what the entry is standardized TO — they are NOT cross-species §8.5 misplacement. Same-species preparation pattern documented in SB-4 / SB-5.
sources: Marx et al. 2017 systematic review on ginger for chemotherapy-induced nausea (PMID 28200047)
USP-NF Ginger monograph (Zingiber officinale rhizome, identification + standardization to gingerols by HPLC)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Ginger (Zingiber officinale, Zingiberaceae) rhizome. UNII C5529G5JPQ. Digestive, antiemetic and anti-inflammatory botanical standardized for gingerols (typically 5%, ranging 5-20% in concentrated extracts) plus shogaols (formed during drying/heating). SB-6 §8.5 / v6 annotation 2026-05-22: refined standard_name to include species; added v6 contract fields (bonus_eligible, standardization_basis, marker_compounds, bonus_rationale, sources); added marker-explicit alias phrasings for direct matching. The audit's reported '5 distinct UNIIs in aliases' resolved to marker-compound UNIIs (V2ZD052TS1, SAS9Z1SVUK, 925QK2Z900) and generic registry term (UL302SFN7L), all describing the same species (Z. officinale, UNII C5529G5JPQ) — not cross-species misplacement.

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Grape seed (Vitis vinifera, UNII RDS2V6DVY5 for the standardized extract preparation; C34U15ICXA for plain seed identity in botanical_ingredients.grape_seed) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares OPC % (typically 90-95% — the high-OPC threshold) OR a branded standardized extract (Masquelier's OPC, MegaNatural-BP). Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'grape seed' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. The audit's reported '5 distinct UNIIs in aliases' reflect marker-compound UNIIs (ZZE3L9H7KH proanthocyanidins, 7G50Y5P93W Masquelier's OPC) and preparation variants — not cross-species §8.5 misplacement.
sources: Sano et al. 2007 RCT on MegaNatural-BP for blood pressure (PMID 17190620)
Bagchi et al. 2003 systematic review on OPC pharmacology and cardiovascular outcomes (PMID 12867234)
standardized_botanicals._sb9_pending_review: Sibling entry 'grape_seed_extract' (id) also declares external_ids.unii='RDS2V6DVY5' and overlaps with this entry's content. Likely a duplicate that warrants merger or one of them becoming the canonical bonus pathway with the other retired. Flagged for a future SB-9b decision; SB-9 only annotates this primary entry to keep scope tight.
standardized_botanicals.notes: Vitis vinifera seed extract (Vitaceae). SB-9 §8.5 / v6 annotation 2026-05-22: added v6 contract fields + marker-explicit alias phrasings + Masquelier's OPC and MegaNatural-BP branded variants. Sibling 'grape_seed_extract' entry overlaps with this one (same UNII); flagged for future merger.

aliases: green coffee bean extract standardized to 45% chlorogenic acids

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Bonus is granted when the product label declares chlorogenic acid % (typically 45-50%) or names a verified branded standardized extract (Svetol). Both Coffea arabica AND Coffea canephora robusta are valid source species — the marker (chlorogenic acid) is what makes the extract bonus-worthy, not species. Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate. Plain 'Coffee Bean Extract' identity (no marker) lives in botanical_ingredients.coffee_bean_plain (no A5b bonus).
sources: NIH ODS Botanical Dietary Supplement Standardization concept (definition of marker-based standardization): https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/list-all/
Naturex Svetol monograph (Coffea canephora robusta, 45-50% chlorogenic acids; original European weight-loss RCTs)
Onakpoya et al. 2011 meta-analysis on green coffee bean extract and weight loss (PMID 21861914) — used Svetol/GCA-standardized preparations
standardized_botanicals.notes: Weight management extract standardized for 45-50% chlorogenic acids (GCA). v6 annotation 2026-05-22: this is the first entry to carry the new schema fields (bonus_eligible / standardization_basis / marker_compounds / bonus_rationale / sources) added under the bonus-eligibility contract. Plain aliases ('green coffee bean extract', 'coffea robusta', etc.) preserved for runtime matching — the enricher's meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148) is the authoritative bonus-eligibility check at scoring time. Shadow-score audit pre-commit confirmed 53/53 products that match this entry today have has_standardized_botanical=True via the percentage detection block; ~50% of spot-checked cases have explicit chlorogenic-acid % evidence in the broader product text (the gate works). Marker (chlorogenic acid) is what makes the extract bonus-worthy, not species — both Coffea arabica and Coffea canephora robusta source variants are valid.

aliases: nettle root extract standardized to 1% beta-sitosterol
nettle root extract standardized to beta-sitosterol
nettle leaf extract standardized to silica
urtica dioica root extract standardized to lignans
urtica dioica root extract standardized to scopoletin
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Nettle (Urtica dioica, UNII 710FLW4U46) bonus-eligibility splits by plant part: nettle ROOT is bonus-eligible when the label declares beta-sitosterol % (typical ~1%), scopoletin %, or a lignans claim — used for BPH / prostate. Nettle LEAF is bonus-eligible when the label declares silica % or a formic-acid claim — used for allergy / joint support. Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Nettle' / 'Urtica dioica' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. Part-specific identity (without marker claim) routes to botanical_ingredients.nettle_root (UNII J8HE8A6E5T) or botanical_ingredients.nettle_leaf (UNII 232L6DS3Y4) for plain identity matching — those entries do NOT trigger the bonus.
sources: Schneider & Rubben 2004 long-term nettle root for BPH (PMID 15211420) — beta-sitosterol pathway
Roschek et al. 2009 randomized double-blind trial of nettle leaf for allergic rhinitis (PMID 19140159) — leaf pathway
ESCOP Nettle Root / Nettle Leaf monographs (separate plant-part standardization criteria)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Nettle (Urtica dioica, Urticaceae). Species-level UNII 710FLW4U46. Two clinically distinct plant parts with different markers and uses: ROOT for BPH/prostate (beta-sitosterol, scopoletin, lignans); LEAF for allergy/joint (silica, formic acid, chlorophyll). SB-7 root-vs-leaf split 2026-05-22: removed part-specific aliases ('nettle root extract', 'nettle leaf extract' — those identify the plant part, not a standardization claim — moved to existing botanical_ingredients.nettle_root and botanical_ingredients.nettle_leaf canonicals which do NOT trigger A5b). Added v6 contract fields acknowledging dual-marker pathway (root markers + leaf markers); added marker-explicit alias phrasings for BOTH pathways so labels with explicit standardization claims (beta-sitosterol %, silica %) match directly. Species-level aliases ('Urtica dioica', 'stinging nettle') retained for runtime matching gated by meets_threshold.
Patented coffee fruit extract shown to increase BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor). [PB-2 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: NeuroFactor® (Futureceuticals) is a clinically-studied whole coffee-fruit extract (Coffea arabica fruit, NOT bean) standardized to a defined polyphenol/chlorogenic-acid profile. Clinical literature reports rapid (1-hour) brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) elevation in healthy adults. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the NeuroFactor® brand. Companion identity in botanical_ingredients.coffee_fruit and the CoffeeBerry® bonus entry standardized_botanicals.coffeeberry. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Botanical ingredient standardized for hydroxytyrosol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.

Branded multiple-berry anthocyanin blend. Raw DSLD OpriBerry(R) rows match the documented OptiBerry composition of cranberry, elderberry, raspberry, strawberry, bilberry, and blueberry/wild blueberry. [PB-1 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: OptiBerry® (InterHealth Nutraceuticals, now Lonza) is a clinically-studied blend of six berry extracts (wild blueberry, wild bilberry, cranberry, elderberry, raspberry seed, strawberry) standardized to high anthocyanin / polyphenol content. Used in antioxidant / anti-angiogenic research. Multi-source blend has no single-substance UNII; the brand name is the bonus trigger. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the OptiBerry® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown if not specified. [PB-1 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).

Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical (score_supplements.py:1148).

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Chemical|Resveratrol | 438 (Number of products:1077)

aliases: rhodiola rosea extract standardized to 3% rosavins, 1% salidroside
rhodiola rosea extract standardized to 3% rosavins
rhodiola rosea extract standardized to 1% salidroside
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Rhodiola rosea (UNII 3S5ITS5ULN) is bonus-eligible when the product label declares the standard 3% rosavins + 1% salidroside specification (or a named branded standardized extract such as SHR-5 from Swedish Herbal Institute, which is the spec used in most adaptogen RCTs). The 3:1 rosavins-to-salidroside ratio is the historical Russian / Scandinavian pharmacopeial standard. Runtime enforcement: enricher's _extract_percentage + meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148). Plain 'Rhodiola' identity matches but defers to meets_threshold. The bonus pathway is species-specific to R. rosea — other Rhodiola species (R. crenulata UNII E1596V65V0, R. quadrifida / R. sacra UNII 11R149C3CY) have different marker profiles and are NOT covered by this entry's pathway. Editorial note: 'rhodiola root' is generic phrasing — UNII registry assigns it to R. quadrifida / R. sacra, but supplement-context labels typically mean R. rosea. Alias retained for runtime matching given that convention; products using non-R. rosea species should call them out explicitly (e.g., 'Rhodiola crenulata').
sources: Panossian et al. 2010 systematic review on Rhodiola rosea pharmacology (PMID 20378318)
Swedish Herbal Institute SHR-5 monograph (Rhodiola rosea standardized to 3% rosavins + 1% salidroside)
Olsson et al. 2009 randomized double-blind trial on SHR-5 for stress-related fatigue (PMID 18307390)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Rhodiola rosea (Crassulaceae) root. UNII 3S5ITS5ULN. Adaptogenic herb standardized for rosavins (rosavin, rosin, rosarin — collectively 'rosavins') at min 3% plus salidroside at min 1%, in the traditional 3:1 ratio. SHR-5 (Swedish Herbal Institute) is the original standardized extract used in adaptogen RCTs. SB-8 §8.5 fix + v6 annotation 2026-05-22: closed the missing-target-UNII gap (entry had empty external_ids; now populated with 3S5ITS5ULN); refined standard_name to species precision; added v6 contract fields + marker-explicit aliases for direct matching of labels carrying the standardization claim. Other Rhodiola species (R. crenulata UNII E1596V65V0, R. quadrifida / R. sacra UNII 11R149C3CY) are different species with different marker profiles — they are NOT covered by this entry's bonus pathway and should route to their own canonicals when products surface using them.
Botanical ingredient standardized for aspalathin. Commonly used in dietary supplements.

standardized_botanicals.standard_name: Sea Buckthorn Fruit Oil (Hippophae rhamnoides fruit oil)
aliases: sea buckthorn fruit oil standardized to omega-7 palmitoleic acid
Hippophae rhamnoides fruit oil standardized to palmitoleic acid
standardized_botanicals.notes: Mesocarp/pulp oil of Hippophae rhamnoides fruit — fatty-acid profile dominated by palmitoleic acid (omega-7) and oleic acid (omega-9), plus carotenoids and tocopherols. UNII TA4JCF9S1J = HIPPOPHAE RHAMNOIDES FRUIT OIL (verified via scripts/data/fda_unii_cache.json). Part-ambiguous mentions ('sea buckthorn oil', 'seabuckthorn oil', 'sea buckthorn extract', 'seaberry') were removed from this entry per §8.5 — they may refer to seed oil, which has materially different chemistry and lives under sea_buckthorn_seed_oil (UNII T53SBG6741). Plain juice/powder identity continues to live in botanical_ingredients.sea_buckthorn.
standardized_botanicals.threshold_source: 3% omega-7 palmitoleic acid — fruit oil standard
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Hippophae rhamnoides fruit oil is commercially standardized to palmitoleic acid (omega-7) content (typically 3%+ for premium grades, with high-grade Siberian/Tibetan extracts at 30%+). Clinical literature on dry-eye, mucous-membrane, and cardiometabolic endpoints uses fruit-oil standardization. The marker_percent basis applies: bonus is granted only when the label proves measurable palmitoleic-acid/omega-7 percentage or quantified constituent standardization. The enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148) enforce label-text percentage detection at runtime.

standardized_botanicals.standard_name: Sea Buckthorn Seed Oil (Hippophae rhamnoides seed oil)
aliases: sea buckthorn seed oil standardized to alpha-linolenic acid
Hippophae rhamnoides seed oil standardized to linoleic and linolenic acids
standardized_botanicals.notes: Seed/kernel oil of Hippophae rhamnoides — fatty-acid profile dominated by alpha-linolenic acid (omega-3) and linoleic acid (omega-6); NOT the palmitoleic-rich fruit oil. UNII T53SBG6741 = HIPPOPHAE RHAMNOIDES SEED OIL (verified via scripts/data/fda_unii_cache.json). Companion to sea_buckthorn (fruit oil, UNII TA4JCF9S1J). Part-ambiguous label text ('sea buckthorn oil') without seed-vs-fruit disambiguation maps to plain identity in botanical_ingredients.sea_buckthorn (no bonus).
standardized_botanicals.threshold_source: 30%+ combined alpha-linolenic + linoleic acid — seed oil standard
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Hippophae rhamnoides seed oil is standardized to its essential-fatty-acid profile (combined alpha-linolenic + linoleic acids typically 60%+ of total fatty acids) and gamma-tocopherol content. Clinical literature on inflammatory and dermatological endpoints uses seed-oil-specific standardization. The marker_percent basis applies: bonus is granted only when the label proves measurable EFA percentage or quantified constituent standardization. The enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148) enforce label-text percentage detection at runtime.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Setria — fermentation-derived L-glutathione by Kyowa Hakko. ≥98% L-glutathione by HPLC.
Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown if not specified. [PB-2 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Sharp-PS® Green (Lipogen) is a soy-derived phosphatidylserine preparation manufactured without hexane (Green process), standardized to 20%+ PS content. Clinical literature on cognitive performance, memory, and exercise stress endpoints. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Sharp-PS® Green brand. Companion plain identity in botanical_ingredients.phosphatidylserine (no bonus). Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Botanical ingredient standardized for 6-shogaol, 8-shogaol. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Botanical ingredient standardized for eleutherosides. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Patented potato protein extract that promotes satiety by triggering CCK release. [PB-2 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Slendesta® (Kemin Industries) is a potato protein extract (Solanum tuberosum) standardized to the PI2 (proteinase inhibitor II) bioactive fraction. Clinical literature on satiety / cholecystokinin elevation / appetite endpoints. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Slendesta® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Slimbione — bitter melon (Momordica charantia) extract. Standardized to charantin + polypeptide-p (proprietary ratio).
Blue-green algae standardized for 15-25% phycocyanin content. Contains 60-70% protein.
Mood support botanical standardized for 0.3% hypericin. Used in mild-to-moderate depression and mood balance supplements. Interacts with many medications.
Botanical ingredient standardized for ecdysterones. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
SunActive Iron — micronized ferric pyrophosphate. High bioavailability iron form. Dosed by mg elemental iron. [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: SunActive® Fe (Taiyo Kagaku) is a micronized, microencapsulated ferric pyrophosphate preparation with improved tolerability and absorption vs traditional iron salts. Clinical literature on iron-deficiency anemia and tolerability endpoints. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the SunActive® brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Botanical ingredient standardized for anthocyanins. Commonly used in dietary supplements.
Thermosil — silicon/silica complex. Limited public standardization data. Requires verification. [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Thermosil is a branded silicon/silica complex marketed for bone and connective-tissue support. Standardized to bioavailable orthosilicic acid content. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the Thermosil brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical. [Note: limited public clinical literature — verification advisory; may downgrade to plain identity in future review.]

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Tribulus | 2012 (Number of products:451)

aliases: turkey tail extract standardized to 30% polysaccharides
Trametes versicolor extract standardized to polysaccharide krestin (PSK)
standardized_botanicals.notes: Immune support mushroom standardized for 30%+ polysaccharides (PSK, PSP). UNII 2YD82VN5CM = TRAMETES VERSICOLOR WHOLE (verified via scripts/data/fda_unii_cache.json). Strain-specific UNII 26BE3YVD39 (CM-101) and fruiting-body UNII 4C900477MT are preparation variants. The 'PSK' and 'PSP' abbreviations collide with unrelated protein UNIIs (XY526V7HUL, LQ0G3D3X8F) in the FDA registry — those are NOT polysaccharide fractions; PSK/PSP here refer to the Trametes versicolor polysaccharide fractions (krestin, polysaccharide peptide / Yun zhi).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Trametes versicolor extracts are clinically and commercially standardized to polysaccharide-fraction content (typically 30%+ polysaccharides; isolated fractions PSK and PSP have decades of oncology-adjunct evidence in Japan and China). The mushroom_fraction basis applies: bonus is granted only when the label proves measurable polysaccharide/PSK/PSP percentage, branded fraction (Krestin/Yun zhi), or quantified constituent standardization. The enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148) enforce label-text percentage detection at runtime.

aliases: curcuma longa extract standardized to curcuminoids
standardized_botanicals.notes: Curcuma longa whole rhizome (UNII 856YO1Z64F = CURCUMA LONGA (TURMERIC) ROOT, verified). Anti-inflammatory spice standardized for 95% curcuminoids when in extract form. Raw turmeric powder contains only 2-5% curcuminoids and will NOT satisfy the runtime meets_threshold gate (score_supplements.py:1148) — plain rhizome aliases (turmeric powder, turmeric root, haldi) stay here so identity routing works, but the bonus fires only when the label proves a measurable curcuminoid percentage. Plain identity also lives in botanical_ingredients.turmeric and botanical_ingredients.turmeric_root_powder. Branded standardized extracts (C3 Complex, BCM-95, Meriva, Longvida, Theracurmin) are catalogued under standardized_botanicals.curcumin.

gsrs.dsld_info_raw: Dietary Supplement Label Database|Botanical|Turmeric | 48 (Number of products:2583)

standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: Turmeric (Curcuma longa rhizome) extract is commercially and clinically standardized to total curcuminoid percentage (typically 95%+ for premium grades). Clinical literature on anti-inflammatory, joint-comfort, and anti-oxidant endpoints uses standardized curcuminoid percentage. The marker_percent basis applies: bonus is granted only when the label proves measurable curcuminoid percentage. Raw rhizome powder (2-5% curcuminoids) routes through identity-only mapping in botanical_ingredients without bonus. The enricher's meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical gates (score_supplements.py:1148) enforce label-text percentage detection at runtime.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Brand/standardized extract added from unmapped list; markers unknown. [PB-2 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: TurmiPure® Gold (Naturex / Givaudan) is a water-dispersible curcumin extract delivering enhanced bioavailability vs unformulated turmeric powder (~85x by AUC). Standardized to curcuminoid content with proprietary delivery system. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names the TurmiPure® Gold brand. Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.

standardized_botanicals.notes: Uniflex (UC-II) — undenatured type II collagen from chicken sternum cartilage (InterHealth/Lonza). Clinical dose 40 mg. Identity by native structure, not % standardization. [PB-3 2026-05-22] Promoted to v6 contract (branded_extract basis).
standardized_botanicals.bonus_rationale: UC-II® / Uniflex (InterHealth Nutraceuticals, now Lonza) is a clinically-studied undenatured type II collagen from chicken sternum cartilage. Identity is by native molecular structure (undenatured collagen helix), not by % standardization — clinical dose is fixed at 40 mg. Clinical literature on joint comfort vs glucosamine+chondroitin comparator. branded_extract basis: bonus is granted when the label names UC-II® or Uniflex brand. No single substance UNII (collagen is structurally-diverse class). Runtime gate: meets_threshold + has_standardized_botanical.
